subject: [PATCH v2 023/110] ceph: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of PRIino format for i_ino, specifically in the ceph_encode_encrypted_dname and ceph_fname_to_usr functions. The author's response is neutral as they are simply re-posting the original patch description without any changes or explanations.

reviews.author: Viacheslav Dubeyko
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch uses PRIino format for i_ino, but did not provide any specific technical concerns or suggestions.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good.

Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <Slava.Dubeyko@ibm.com>

Thanks,
Slava.
______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the Ceph file system to use the PRIino format macro for printing inode numbers, which will be widened in a future change. The update also corrects signed format specifiers to unsigned, as inode numbers are unsigned values.
